untranslatable_reason: ABW (Ascending Broadening Wedge) is a complex geometric price pattern requiring multi-bar shape recognition, trend line construction, and partial move detection within the pattern boundaries. The allowed indicators cannot directly detect wedge formations, intrapattern partial rises/declines, or pattern-relative price crossing events. Pattern detection requires specialized charting logic beyond single-bar technical indicators.
translation_notes: Signal describes partial price moves within a wedge pattern structure. Entry conditions depend on identifying: (1) partial rises in bull context, (2) partial declines in bear context, (3) price crossing within pattern during these moves. These require pattern geometry analysis not available in the allowed indicator set. A practical workaround using pivot crossovers is suggested but does not capture the true ABW signal mechanics. Author silent on bull success rate and specific exit rules; used pattern reversal as fallback exit. Volume gap condition noted but not quantifiable without pattern context.
